title: Socceroos star Cristian Volpato faces drug-related charges after alleged positive cocaine test
dek: Cristian Volpato, a 22-year-old Australian midfielder who appeared in the World Cup in July 2026, allegedly tested positive for cocaine during a roadside drug test after being stopped for speeding on Sydney's Anzac Bridge at 1:10 AM on Friday. His international driver's licence was suspended for six months. A secondary oral fluid sample was taken for further analysis.
author: The GoCheckMySports Desk
key_fact: A World Cup player faces drug-related charges following a traffic stop in Sydney, raising questions about athlete conduct and legal consequences for a rising talent.
bottom_line: The results of the secondary oral fluid sample analysis and any formal charges will determine the scope of legal and sporting consequences Volpato faces. Whether this incident affects his eligibility or participation with the Socceroos or Sassuolo remains unclear pending official statements from Football Australia and the club.
body: Cristian Volpato, a 22-year-old midfielder who represented Australia at the World Cup in July 2026, allegedly tested positive for cocaine in a roadside drug test following a traffic stop early Friday morning on Sydney's Anzac Bridge. The incident has triggered drug-related charges and raised questions about athlete conduct and legal consequences for a player who recently switched international allegiance from Italy to Australia.

Police stopped Volpato at 1:10 AM on Friday after he was clocked at 109 km/h on the Anzac Bridge, nearly 50 km/h over the designated speed limit, according to ESPN's reporting. He was driving a BMW at the time. A roadside drug test allegedly returned a positive result for cocaine, and a secondary sample of oral fluid was taken for further analysis. A roadside breath test returned a negative result.

Volpato's international driver's licence was suspended for six months as a result of the incident. The player had also been stopped the previous evening at 11:05 PM on Thursday for travelling between 86 and 94 km/h in a 60 km/h zone and was issued a fine.

Volpato made three appearances for the Socceroos at the World Cup after switching his international eligibility from Italy, where he had represented the national team at U19 and U20 levels. Born and raised in Sydney, he played junior football at Sydney United 58, Sydney FC, and Western Sydney before moving overseas. He currently plays club football for Italian side Sassuolo, where he has scored seven goals in three seasons after two seasons with Roma. In March 2026, before Italy's World Cup elimination, Volpato said he was waiting for Italy to select him for the senior side.

NSW Police reported the incident on Friday, per ESPN. Football Australia was contacted for comment but has not yet issued an official response. Several open questions remain: the results of the secondary oral fluid sample analysis, whether Volpato has been formally charged with any offence, what legal consequences he may face, and what disciplinary action, if any, Sassuolo or the Socceroos will take.
